items.title: Swap pip for uv to cut install and CI time
items.why: uv is a Rust drop-in for pip/venv that resolves and installs dependencies in a fraction of the time, so local setup and every cold-cache CI run get dramatically faster.
how_to_steps: Install uv (`curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh` or `pip install uv`), then confirm with `uv --version`.
In the project, run `uv init` if there is no pyproject.toml, then `uv add -r requirements.txt` to import existing deps and generate uv.lock.
Commit pyproject.toml and uv.lock; recreate environments with `uv sync` and run commands via `uv run <cmd>` instead of activating a venv.
Swap the CI install step to `uv sync --frozen` for deterministic, cache-fast installs, and compare the install stage time before/after.
items.evidence: Real Python measured ~8x faster than pip (21.4s -> 2.6s installing JupyterLab); a widely cited uncached install dropped 38s -> 3s; one team cut a five-project CI suite from ~9 min to ~4.5 min.
items.try_today: Run `uv sync` on one repo and switch its CI install step to uv, then compare the install stage timing.
